notes.type_correctness: All WS message types are correctly defined in server/types.ts and the WsMessage union is complete with all 5 new chat types. DB row types and client ChatConversationSummary/ChatMessage interfaces match exactly.
notes.pattern_adherence: ChatManager follows the existing spawn+readline pattern from QueueManager. One stale closure bug was found and fixed in useChat.ts createConversation — the setActiveTabIndex callback was capturing conversations.length from the outer scope instead of using the functional updater pattern.
notes.test_coverage: All 6 ChatManager test cases pass: sendMessage, abort, command proposals, deduplication, session_id storage, and isActive lifecycle. No client-side tests exist, which is consistent with the rest of the client codebase.
notes.security: ChatManager spawns 'claude' with --dangerously-skip-permissions on the server. This is the same risk posture as QueueManager. The claudeOnPath() check prevents clear error paths but does not sandbox the subprocess. The SYSTEM_PROMPT is hardcoded and not user-configurable.
notes.architectural_alignment: SharedWebSocketProvider correctly lifts the single WS connection above Routes in App.tsx. ChatPanel is mounted in RootLayout ensuring it survives navigation. The chat API reuses existing db.ts patterns for migration and CRUD. REST routes follow the established Express pattern with 201/202/404/409 status codes.
